commentary_html:
1. Global Macro Pulse
MarketWatch reports Friday's strong jobs report could pressure the Fed, noting "The Fed may already be too late in hiking interest rates," which poses challenges for borrowers struggling with inflation. CNBC reports the U.S. has proposed fresh tariffs on 60 economies over forced labor trade practices, with rates of 10-12.5%. Geopolitically, CNBC notes U.S.-Iran tensions are intensifying as "ceasefire frays, peace talks stall," following a Kuwait airport attack.

2. Sector & Market Outlook
According to CNBC, Meta shares sank on an FT report the company could raise "tens of billions of dollars" via stock offering to fund its AI push, while the Trump administration and OpenAI are discussing a possible government stake in the AI startup. Bloomberg reports Wall Street has soured on Lululemon after the company cut its annual sales outlook, with only one analyst still rating it a buy. MarketWatch notes an S&P 500 rebalancing is imminent, with Marvell, Reddit, and a mortgage company potentially earning index inclusion.

3. Portfolio & Watchlist View
CNBC reports Google will pay SpaceX $920 million per month for compute capacity at xAI data centers—our watchlist candidate RDW (Redwire) operates in the space sector, though this headline does not directly reference the company. Our semiconductor-adjacent holding ARM could see indirect sentiment shifts from the broader AI infrastructure spending news (Meta, OpenAI), but no direct news on ARM appears in today's headlines. No other holdings or watchlist names are explicitly mentioned.

4. Risk Radar
🔴 Rate Sensitivity: MarketWatch warns strong jobs data may force Fed rate hikes, directly pressuring growth stocks and borrowers.
🔴 Geopolitical Escalation: CNBC reports U.S.-Iran attacks are intensifying as ceasefire agreements fray.
🟡 Crypto Contagion: CNBC reports Bitcoin cracked below $60,000 to October 2024 lows as yields rose, signaling risk-off sentiment spreading.

⚠️ AI-generated analysis based on news headlines. Verify all claims independently before making trading decisions.
